1: The summons to the nations (goyim) and peoples (leumim) is a universal call to witness -- come near and hear. The scope expands: not just nations but the earth and everything in it, the world and all that comes from it. The four concentric expansions (nations, peoples, earth, world) make the oracle explicitly universal in its audience even before the specific target (Edom, v5) is named. This is divine judgment that the entire creation must witness.
2: YHWH is flagged -- the LORD is enraged against all the nations. The divine wrath is directed at all (kol) nations and all their armies -- the universality matched by the comprehensive judgment: he has devoted them to destruction (heherimam: placed them under the ban of holy war), given them over to slaughter. The cherem (devoted to destruction) is the most absolute form of divine judgment -- everything under cherem is given entirely to the LORD and cannot be retained.
3: The physical consequences of cherem: the slain cast out unburied, the stench of decomposing corpses rising, the mountains soaked with blood. The scale of judgment is matched by the scale of the physical aftermath. The mountains drinking blood is a hyperbole that captures the comprehensive character of the slaughter -- even the landscape is saturated with the results of divine judgment.
4: The cosmic-dissolution language: all the host of heaven dissolving, the heavens rolled up like a scroll, the stars falling like leaves or figs. Revelation 6:13-14 echoes this exactly: stars falling, sky rolling back like a scroll. The MKT's rolled up like a scroll preserves the exact image that Revelation cites. The creaturely heavens (not the divine throne) are described as temporary -- the cosmos can be un-created as decisively as it was created.
5: YHWH's sword bathed in heaven -- the divine sword is described as already prepared in the heavenly realm before it descends on Edom. The people devoted to destruction (cherem) are specifically identified as Edom. The sword descending from heaven to earth for judgment is the pattern NT apocalyptic uses for Christ's return with the sword from his mouth (Revelation 19:15). The MKT's bathed in heaven is literal -- the sword is dripping from its heavenly preparation.
6: YHWH is flagged twice -- the sword is the LORD's, filled with blood from Edomite sacrificial animals. The sacrifice imagery (lambs, goats, rams, fat, kidneys) makes the Edomite slaughter a divine sacrifice on a massive scale -- as if Edom were being offered to the LORD in the same way Israel offered sacrifices. The LORD has a sacrifice in Bozrah (the Edomite capital). This sacrificial-slaughter language recurs in Revelation 19:17-18 for the eschatological battle.
7: Wild oxen, young bulls, and mighty bulls falling alongside the Edom armies -- the wild animals of the land participate in the judgment, soaked with blood along with the human armies. The fertility of the land (fat and rich soil) is inverted: instead of grain from productive soil, blood is absorbed into the soil. The MKT's soaked with blood and enriched with fat follow the Hebrew's agricultural inversion.
8: YHWH is flagged -- the LORD has a day of vengeance, a year of recompense for the cause of Zion. The double time-span (day, year) indicates both the decisive moment and the extended period of the LORD's judgment. Cause of Zion (riv Tzion: the legal case/quarrel of Zion) introduces the forensic dimension: this is not arbitrary violence but legal retribution for what was done to Zion. The LORD acts as Zion's legal advocate and enforcer.
9: The environmental transformation of Edom's territory -- streams become tar, soil becomes sulfur, land becomes burning pitch. The Sodom-and-Gomorrah pattern (fire and sulfur, Genesis 19:24-25) is applied to Edom, making Edom's judgment a second Sodom. The MKT's tar and burning pitch preserve the combustible-liquid imagery that makes the land itself flammable and permanently hostile to life.
10: Olam (everlasting, disp 3) is flagged -- its smoke will rise forever. The MKT renders olam as forever -- absolute permanent duration. Revelation 14:11 applies this exactly to the smoke of torment rising forever and ever for those who worship the beast. The permanent desolation of Edom -- from generation to generation it will lie desolate -- functions in Revelation as the type for permanent eschatological judgment.
11: YHWH is flagged -- the LORD stretches over Edom the measuring line of tohu (formlessness) and the plumb line of bohu (emptiness). These are the exact words of Genesis 1:2 (the earth was tohu vabohu -- formless and void) before creation. The LORD is un-creating Edom, measuring it out with the tools of Genesis but using them to produce pre-creation chaos rather than ordered creation. The MKT's measuring line of chaos and plumb line of desolation captures this de-creation inversion.
12: They will call it No Kingdom There, and all its princes will come to nothing. The renaming of the land as No Kingdom There is the linguistic embodiment of the un-creation: the political reality (kingdom, princes) is declared non-existent. The nothingness (efes) that characterizes the princes is the same word for zero/nothing used for the idols and nations in Isaiah 40-41.
13: Thorns overtaking citadels, nettles and brambles in fortresses -- the reversal of human architectural achievement by wild vegetation. Haunt for jackals and home for owls: the wilderness animals that inhabit desolate places replace the human inhabitants. The citadels and fortresses that were Edom's pride become nesting grounds for scavengers.
14: The night owl (lilit) will settle there and find herself a resting place. The lilit is a creature of the desert and desolation -- variously rendered as screech owl, night creature, or (in later tradition) a demonic figure. The MKT's night owl is consistent with the surrounding list of desert animals and is a defensible naturalistic rendering. The settling and finding of rest is ironic: the desolate land provides rest for the creatures of desolation.
15: The great owl makes her nest, lays eggs, hatches, gathers young -- the complete reproductive cycle happening in the former citadel. The arrows-gather metaphor (dayyah, possibly a falcon or kite) gathering her kind adds another predatory bird. The thoroughness of the animal colonization -- nesting, breeding, gathering -- shows that the desolation is not temporary but has become the permanent habitat of wilderness creatures.
16: YHWH is flagged; ruach (Spirit, disp 4) is flagged -- for my mouth has commanded it, and his Spirit has gathered them. The book of the LORD is searched for verification that each animal of vv11-15 is present -- not one will be absent, not one will lack its mate. His Spirit gathered them connects the creative-gathering work of the Spirit (Genesis 1:2) to the de-creative gathering of desolation-animals. The same Spirit who gathered creation now gathers the un-creation. The MKT capitalizes Spirit here, reflecting the divine agent.
17: Olam (everlasting, disp 3) is flagged twice -- they will possess it forever; from generation to generation they will dwell in it. The animals' permanent possession of the desolated land is expressed with two time-spanning phrases. The MKT's forever and from generation to generation follow the Hebrew's paired temporal expressions. The lot-casting and measuring-line division (v17) mirrors the conquest-era land distribution of Israel -- but now used for the permanent allotment of wilderness to wilderness-creatures.
